name: Nuo Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Nuo Therapeutics, Inc. is a commercial-stage medical device company focused on developing and marketing autologous platelet-rich plasma (PRP) therapy for chronic wound care primarily in the U.S. Its only significant commercial offering is the Aurix System, a point-of-care device that separates a patient's blood to produce a platelet-based gel for wound management. The company sells Aurix to hospital outpatient wound care clinics and private practice physicians, and also supplies a private-label version to Smith+Nephew under a distribution agreement.
sellsTo: private practice physicians treating chronic wounds

exposures.sentence: We are a commercial-stage medical device company focused on developing and marketing products for chronic wound care primarily within the U.S.

catalysts.event: Entry into Distribution Agreement with Smith+Nephew
catalysts.sentence: On March 31, 2025, we entered into a Distribution Agreement (the Distribution Agreement ) with a U.S. affiliate of Smith & Nephew PLC ( Smith+Nephew ), a global medical technology company.

catalysts.event: First commercial sales of Private Label product under Distribution Agreement
catalysts.sentence: During the initial term of the Distribution Agreement commencing on the date of first commercial sales in October 2025, minimum annual purchase commitments will apply to Smith+Nephew of an average of approximately $500,000 per year for Smith+Nephew to maintain exclusive distribution rights.

catalysts.event: Shelf registration statement on Form S-3 declared effective
catalysts.sentence: On May 12, 2025, the SEC declared our shelf registration statement on Form S-3 effective.

catalysts.event: Entry into Loan and Security Agreement and Initial Funding
catalysts.sentence: On January 21, 2026, we entered into a Loan and Security Agreement (the Loan Agreement ) with four lenders... The closing of the Initial Funding occurred on January 23, 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Advanced wound care market estimated at approximately $10.8 billion globally in 2021 and North American market approximately $4.15 billion in 2020.
tamClaims.sentence: The advanced wound care market, within which Aurix competes, is composed of advanced wound care dressings, wound care devices, and wound care biologics, and was estimated to be an approximately $10.8 billion global market in 2021 with the North American market estimated at approximately $4.15 billion in 2020.

tamClaims.claim: Treatment of diabetic foot ulcers cost an estimated $9-$13 billion annually in the U.S. alone.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Institute of Health, treatment of diabetic foot ulcers cost an estimated $9-$13 billion annually in the U.S. alone.
